rationale: The score is identical (3/10) in both evaluations and the reasoning is materially the same: both flag the lack of native Japanese, embedded firmware/LAN-switch experience, C-on-UNIX recency, and Linux kernel work. The textual differences (e.g., listing specific languages, mentioning GIS infrastructure) are ordinary rewording and run-to-run variation, not penalties tied to the São Paulo/Brazil location. Nothing in the variant keys off the address change.
